articles.title: China’s Z.ai claims it can match Mythos on cybersecurity
articles.subtitle: China's Zhipu AI (Z.ai) released its open-weight GLM-5.2, and some researchers have claimed that it matches Mythos in certain bug-finding and cybersecurity scenarios. While GLM lags behind models from Anthropic and OpenAI in other, more gen
articles.author: Terrence O’Brien
articles.summary: China's Zhipu AI (Z.ai) released its open-weight GLM-5.2, and some researchers have claimed that it matches Mythos in certain bug-finding and cybersecurity scenarios. While GLM lags behind models from Anthropic and OpenAI in other, more general tasks, it seems that China has dramatically reduced the gap in the capabilities between its models and those of […]
body: GLM-5.2 can’t match Anthropic or OpenAI on general tasks, but the gap has closed on finding bugs.

China’s Zhipu AI ( Z.ai ) released its open-weight GLM-5.2, and some researchers have claimed that it matches Mythos in certain bug-finding and cybersecurity scenarios. While GLM lags behind models from Anthropic and OpenAI in other, more general tasks, it seems that China has dramatically reduced the gap in the capabilities between its models and those of the US.

This level of advancement is particularly concerning to the US government, which has worked to restrict China’s access to powerful models like Anthropic’s Mythos and Fable, as well as the hardware necessary to train and run them. The Trump administration views Mythos and other advanced AI models capable of identifying vulnerabilities as serious national security threats. Recently, OpenAI unveiled GPT-5.6 , which has also raised concerns about its potential for misuse and has limited access to it.

Because GLM is an open-weight model, it can be downloaded and run by anyone on readily available hardware. That gives it great flexibility and allows power users deep access, but it also makes it ripe for abuse by bad actors who can run it with little oversight.
keyPoints: GLM-5.2 can’t match Anthropic or OpenAI on general tasks, but the gap has closed on finding bugs.

China’s Zhipu AI ( Z.ai ) released its open-weight GLM-5.2, and some researchers have claimed that it matches Mythos in certain bug-finding and cybersecurity scenarios. While GLM lags behind models from Anthropic and OpenAI in other, more general tasks, it seems that China has dramatically reduced the gap in the capabilities between its models and those of the US.

This level of advancement is particularly concerning to the US government, which has worked to restrict China’s access to powerful models like Anthropic’s Mythos and Fable, as well as the hardware necessary to train and run them. The Trump administration views Mythos and other advanced AI models capable of identifying vulnerabilities as serious national security threats. Recently, OpenAI unveiled GPT-5.6 , which has also raised concerns about its potential for misuse and has limited access to it.
paragraphTranslations: GLM-5.2 在通用任务上无法与 Anthropic 或 OpenAI 匹敌，但在发现漏洞方面的差距已经缩小。
中国的 Zhipu AI（Z.ai）发布了其开放权重的 GLM-5.2，一些研究人员声称它在某些漏洞查找和网络安全场景中与 Mythos 相匹配。虽然 GLM 在其他更通用的任务上落后于 Anthropic 和 OpenAI 的模型，但看来中国已经大幅缩小了其模型与美国模型之间的能力差距。
这种进步水平尤其令美国政府担忧，美国政府一直在努力限制中国获取像 Anthropic 的 Mythos 和 Fable 这样的强大模型，以及训练和运行它们所需的硬件。特朗普政府将 Mythos 和其他能够识别漏洞的先进 AI 模型视为严重的国家安全威胁。最近，OpenAI 推出了 GPT-5.6，这也引发了对其潜在滥用的担忧，并限制了对其的访问。
由于 GLM 是一个开放权重模型，任何人都可以下载并在现成的硬件上运行它。这给了它很大的灵活性，并允许高级用户深入访问，但它也使其容易被不良行为者滥用，他们可以在几乎没有监督的情况下运行它。

paragraphs: China's Zhipu AI, also known as Z.ai, has launched its open-weight GLM-5.2 model. Some researchers say it can match Mythos in bug-finding and cybersecurity. Although GLM-5.2 is not as advanced as models from Anthropic and OpenAI in general tasks, it has significantly reduced the gap between Chinese and US AI capabilities. This advancement worries the US government, which has tried to limit China's access to powerful AI models like Mythos and the hardware needed to run them.
paragraphTranslations: 中国的智谱人工智能（Z.ai）发布了其开放式的GLM-5.2模型。一些研究人员表示，它在查找漏洞和网络安全方面可以与Mythos相匹敌。尽管GLM-5.2在一般任务上不如Anthropic和OpenAI的模型先进，但它已显著缩小了中美人工智能能力的差距。这一进步令美国政府感到担忧，美国政府试图限制中国获取像Mythos这样的强大人工智能模型以及运行它们的硬件。

articles.title: Pakistani strikes kill at least 28 civilians in Afghanistan, UN says
articles.subtitle: Pakistan claims it was targeting militants near its border, but the Taliban government calls it an "atrocity".
articles.author: BBC World
articles.summary: Pakistan claims it was targeting militants near its border, but the Taliban government calls it an "atrocity".
body: Most of the casualties were in the village of Mandokhail, officials say
At least 28 civilians were killed after Pakistan launched air strikes and sent ground troops into Afghan provinces along its border on Sunday, the United Nations Afghan mission has said.
A further 49 were injured and women and children were among the victims, according to Unama.
Afghanistan's Taliban government said civilian homes were hit and described the attack as a "cowardly act" and an "atrocity".
Meanwhile, Pakistan said it had targeted militant hideouts in Afghanistan's Paktia, Paktika and Kunar provinces.
The neighbouring countries agreed to a ceasefire last October following weeks of deadly clashes - an agreement that has since fallen apart.
Casualties were concentrated in Mandokhail, a village in the Paktia province, according to Taliban officials.
Adam Khan, 63, told AFP news agency he "cannot put into words the condition of the children I saw at the hospital, or the screams of their parents and siblings".
Those killed in one of the strikes included "children, elderly people and women" sleeping in a house, he said.
Afghanistan's Taliban government put the civilian death toll at 36 and said more than 160 had been injured.
Pakistan's information minister Attaullah Tarar said 29 militants had been killed in an operation responding to "recent terrorist attacks against innocent people".
The BBC has not independently confirmed figures from either side.
keyPoints: Most of the casualties were in the village of Mandokhail, officials say
At least 28 civilians were killed after Pakistan launched air strikes and sent ground troops into Afghan provinces along its border on Sunday, the United Nations Afghan mission has said.
A further 49 were injured and women and children were among the victims, according to Unama.
paragraphTranslations: 官员们说，大多数伤亡发生在曼多凯尔村。
联合国阿富汗代表团表示，周日巴基斯坦发动空袭并派遣地面部队进入阿富汗边境沿线省份，造成至少28名平民死亡。
根据联合国阿富汗援助团的数据，另有49人受伤，妇女和儿童也在受害者之列。
阿富汗塔利班政府表示平民住宅遭到袭击，并将此次袭击描述为“懦弱行为”和“暴行”。
与此同时，巴基斯坦表示，它已针对阿富汗帕克蒂亚、帕克蒂卡和库纳尔省的好战分子藏身之处。
这两个邻国在去年十月同意停火，结束了数周的致命冲突——但该协议后来破裂了。
据塔利班官员称，伤亡集中在帕克蒂亚省的曼多凯尔村。
63岁的亚当·汗告诉法新社，他“无法用言语形容我在医院看到的孩子们的状况，或者他们父母和兄弟姐妹的尖叫声”。
他说，在一次袭击中丧生的人包括在一所房子里睡觉的“儿童、老人和妇女”。
阿富汗塔利班政府将平民死亡人数定为36人，并表示有超过160人受伤。
巴基斯坦信息部长阿塔拉·塔拉尔表示，在回应“针对无辜人民的最近恐怖袭击”的行动中有29名武装分子被杀。
BBC尚未独立证实双方提供的数字。

vocabulary.example: The war caused many casualties.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 战争造成了许多伤亡。

vocabulary.example: The government condemned the terrorist attack.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 政府谴责了这次恐怖袭击。

vocabulary.example: The war was filled with atrocities.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 这场战争充满了暴行。

vocabulary.example: The militant group claimed responsibility for the attack.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 激进组织声称对这次袭击负责。

vocabulary.example: The police searched for the criminal's hideouts.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 警方搜寻罪犯的藏身之处。

vocabulary.example: The two countries agreed on a ceasefire.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 两国就停火达成一致。

paragraphs: The United Nations Afghan mission reported that at least 28 civilians were killed and 49 injured in Pakistani air strikes and ground troop operations in Afghanistan. The strikes targeted Afghan provinces along the Pakistan border, with most casualties in the village of Mandokhail. Women and children were among the victims. Afghanistan's Taliban government condemned the attack as a 'cowardly act' and an 'atrocity', claiming that civilian homes were hit. Pakistan claimed to have targeted militant hideouts in response to recent terrorist attacks.
paragraphTranslations: 联合国阿富汗任务报告称，巴基斯坦在阿富汗边境沿线的空袭和地面部队行动中至少造成28名平民死亡，49人受伤。空袭主要针对阿富汗的帕克蒂亚、帕克提卡和库纳尔省，其中曼多凯尔村的伤亡最为集中。受害者中包括妇女和儿童。阿富汗塔利班政府谴责这次袭击是“懦弱行为”和“暴行”，声称平民住宅遭到袭击。巴基斯坦声称，他们针对的是激进分子藏身之处，以回应最近针对无辜平民的恐怖袭击。

The strikes targeted Afghan provinces along the Pakistan border, with most casualties in the village of Mandokhail. Women and children were among the victims. Afghanistan's Taliban government condemned the attack as a 'cowardly act' and an 'atrocity', claiming that civilian homes were hit. Pakistan claimed to have targeted militant hideouts in response to recent terrorist attacks.
